images.alt: Rath & Wright's buffalo hide yard in 1878, showing 40,000 buffalo hides, Dodge City, Kansas
images.caption: Rath & Wright's buffalo hide yard, 1878, 40,000 buffalo hides, Dodge City, Kansas.

images.alt: A herd of plains buffalo at Buffalo National Park, Wainwright, Alberta, early 1900s
images.caption: A herd of plains buffalo at Buffalo National Park, Wainwright, Alberta. The survivors of near-extinction, gathered in a protected sanctuary. (Bell Photo, Library and Archives Canada, PA-005127)
images.credit: Bell Photo / Library and Archives Canada

images.alt: Métis freighting brigade with Red River cart and canvas lodge on the open prairie
images.caption: A Métis freighting brigade on the open prairie. The Red River cart and canvas lodge were the backbone of the fur trade economy. (Library and Archives Canada)

images.alt: Hunters standing over dead buffalo, Scotty Philip's herd, Fort Pierre, South Dakota
images.caption: Hunters with the carcasses of buffalo, Fort Pierre, South Dakota. (LCCN 2006689761)

images.alt: Canadian Pacific boxcar loaded with buffalo bones on the prairie
images.caption: The railroad made mass transport of hides and bones profitable. A Canadian Pacific boxcar loaded with buffalo bones on the plains, late 1880s.
images.credit: Glenbow Museum / Canadian Pacific Railway Archives
images.license: Educational use / contact Glenbow for reproduction

images.alt: The Herd drawing from 1860/1913 showing vast buffalo herds
images.caption: "The Herd" drawing, 1860/1913. Context for the scale of slaughter that followed. (AAB)

images.alt: I.G. Baker Company trade store and ledger records, Fort MacLeod
images.caption: I.G. Baker Company records show the peak and collapse of the hide trade. Fort MacLeod, 1874–1879.
images.credit: Glenbow Museum / I.G. Baker Company Archives

images.alt: Eight-man buffalo hunt skinning a carcass, 1870s
images.caption: Buffalo hunt party skinning carcasses. LCCN 2006688689.

images.alt: Pile of bison skulls on the prairie, 1870s
images.caption: Gathering buffalo bones on the prairie. The skulls told the story the living no longer could.

images.alt: Gull Lake, Saskatchewan, 1890 — bone trade era
images.caption: Gull Lake, Saskatchewan, 1890. Bone trade collection point on the CPR line.

images.alt: Stack of buffalo skulls at railway siding, Saskatchewan, ca. 1890
images.caption: Stack of buffalo skulls at a railway siding, Saskatchewan, ca. 1890. (Glenbow Museum)

images.alt: Field of buffalo bones near Lloydminster, Saskatchewan, at Chief Poundmaker's last great corral, ca. 1874
images.caption: Buffalo bones near Lloydminster, Saskatchewan — Chief Poundmaker's last great corral, ca. 1874. The sea of bone stretches to the horizon. (Ernest Brown, copyright)

images.alt: Buffalo hunt in Taylor County, 1874, from historical publication
images.caption: Buffalo Hunt in Taylor County, 1874. (pg. 3 of historical publication)

images.alt: Historical photograph titled 'The Beginning of Better Things'
images.caption: "The Beginning of Better Things" — historical photograph, context uncertain.

images.alt: Another view of buffalo herd at Buffalo National Park, Wainwright, Alberta
images.caption: Buffalo herd at Buffalo National Park, Wainwright, Alberta. (Library and Archives Canada, PA-005106)

images.alt: Looking up the Winnipeg River from Fort Alexander, May 18, 1889
images.caption: Looking up the Winnipeg River from Fort Alexander, 18/5/89. (HBCA 1987/13/1-113, James McDougall album)
